summary.intent: Fix LinkedIn session cookies not persisting across browser container restarts in the Moltis browser pool.
summary.outcome: Added --restore-session-cookies Chrome flag to both the pool.rs launch args and the container.rs CHROME_FLAGS env var, then committed and pushed.

repo: Shared browser profile directory is at ~/.moltis/browser/profile/sandbox/default/
Container stop uses docker/container stop with SIGTERM and 10s grace period for graceful Chrome shutdown
The browserless/chrome container accepts Chrome flags via CHROME_FLAGS environment variable, not direct CLI args
Chrome flags are set in two places: pool.rs (direct launch) and container.rs (containerized launch via env var)

code.finding: Chrome launch args are configured here; --restore-session-cookies was added
Container launch args including CHROME_FLAGS env var are configured here; --restore-session-cookies was added via CHROME_FLAGS

workflow: Chrome session cookies (used by LinkedIn) only persist if Chrome exits gracefully; force-kill loses them
--restore-session-cookies flag is required for session cookies to survive Chrome restarts
Cookie file presence alone is insufficient to confirm persistence — Chrome must exit gracefully to flush session cookies

friction: Chrome flags are split across two files (pool.rs and container.rs), requiring changes in both places for a single behavioral fix
Diagnosing cookie loss required ruling out multiple causes: force-kill, singleton cleanup, and missing Chrome flags
open_items: Verify that Apple Container's stop command actually waits the full 10s grace period for Chrome to flush cookies
Confirm that --restore-session-cookies flag is correctly passed through CHROME_FLAGS in the browserless/chrome container image
